revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を...」
revision.text:
'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要==
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html 道鏡ゆかり、70ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定]読売オンライン（2017年02月09日）2017年02月11日閲覧</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ360尺（約109m））が<ref>[http://www.shokoku-ji.jp/h_siryou_nana.html 歴史資料・相国寺七重大塔]臨済宗相国寺派ホームページ 2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c 金閣寺・敷地内から装飾品出土、七重塔「北山大塔」部材か] 毎日新聞（2016年7月8日）2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した。
==東大寺七重塔の復元== [[1970年]]、[[日本万国博覧会]]の古河パビリオンは、東大寺東塔を再現したものであった。このパビリオンは、博覧会終了後に取り壊されている。
東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>[http://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html 七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から] 産経新聞west（2013年4月26日）2017年2月11日 </ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== {{Commons|Category:Pagodas|仏塔}} {{Commons|Category:Pagodas in Japan|日本の仏塔}} *[[高層建築物]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[多宝塔]] *[[宝塔]] *[[五輪塔]] *[[宝篋印塔]] *[[パゴダ]] *[[相輪塔|相輪橖]] *[[宝珠]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ななしゆうのとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|240px|right|thumb|神奈川県[[海老名中央公園]]内に復元された相模国分寺の七重塔]]
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html 道鏡ゆかり、70ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定]読売オンライン（2017年02月09日）2017年02月11日閲覧</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ360尺（約109m））が<ref>[http://www.shokoku-ji.jp/h_siryou_nana.html 歴史資料・相国寺七重大塔]臨済宗相国寺派ホームページ 2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c 金閣寺・敷地内から装飾品出土、七重塔「北山大塔」部材か] 毎日新聞（2016年7月8日）2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した。
{{-}}
==東大寺七重塔の復元== [[1970年]]、[[日本万国博覧会]]の古河パビリオンは、東大寺東塔を再現したものであった。このパビリオンは、博覧会終了後に取り壊されている。
東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>[http://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html 七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から] 産経新聞west（2013年4月26日）2017年2月11日 </ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== {{Commons|Category:Pagodas|仏塔}} {{Commons|Category:Pagodas in Japan|日本の仏塔}} *[[高層建築物]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[多宝塔]] *[[宝塔]] *[[五輪塔]] *[[宝篋印塔]] *[[パゴダ]] *[[相輪塔|相輪橖]] *[[宝珠]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ななしゆうのとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

revision.comment: 相国寺の塔と北山山荘の塔は移設再建の関係にある同じ塔なのでその辺を修正。
revision.text:
'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|240px|right|thumb|神奈川県[[海老名中央公園]]内に復元された相模国分寺の七重塔]]
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html 道鏡ゆかり、70ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定]読売オンライン（2017年02月09日）2017年02月11日閲覧</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ360尺（約109m））が<ref>[http://www.shokoku-ji.jp/h_siryou_nana.html 歴史資料・相国寺七重大塔]臨済宗相国寺派ホームページ 2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c 金閣寺・敷地内から装飾品出土、七重塔「北山大塔」部材か] 毎日新聞（2016年7月8日）2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した（なお、金閣寺の北山大塔は義満の命令で焼失した相国寺の七重大塔を移転再建したもので、義満の死後に金閣寺の北山大塔が焼失すると、今度は[[足利義持]]の意向で相国寺の元の場所で七重大塔が再建されたという経緯があるため、両者は同じ塔と言える<ref>原田正俊「相国寺の創建と足利義満の仏寺法会」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref><ref>冨島義幸「相国寺七重塔とその伽藍」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref>）。
{{-}}
==東大寺七重塔の復元== [[1970年]]、[[日本万国博覧会]]の古河パビリオンは、東大寺東塔を再現したものであった。このパビリオンは、博覧会終了後に取り壊されている。
東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>[http://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html 七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から] 産経新聞west（2013年4月26日）2017年2月11日 </ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== {{Commons|Category:Pagodas|仏塔}} {{Commons|Category:Pagodas in Japan|日本の仏塔}} *[[高層建築物]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[多宝塔]] *[[宝塔]] *[[五輪塔]] *[[宝篋印塔]] *[[パゴダ]] *[[相輪塔|相輪橖]] *[[宝珠]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ななしゆうのとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:信仰塔]]

{{-}}
==東大寺七重塔の復元== [[1970年]]、[[日本万国博覧会]]の古河パビリオンは、東大寺東塔を再現したものであった。このパビリオンは、博覧会終了後に取り壊されている。
東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>[http://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html 七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から] 産経新聞west（2013年4月26日）2017年2月11日 </ref>。

revision.text:
'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|240px|right|thumb|神奈川県[[海老名中央公園]]内に復元された相模国分寺の七重塔]]
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html 道鏡ゆかり、70ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定]読売オンライン（2017年02月09日）2017年02月11日閲覧</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ360尺（約109m））が<ref>[http://www.shokoku-ji.jp/h_siryou_nana.html 歴史資料・相国寺七重大塔]臨済宗相国寺派ホームページ 2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c 金閣寺・敷地内から装飾品出土、七重塔「北山大塔」部材か] 毎日新聞（2016年7月8日）2017年2月11日閲覧</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した（なお、金閣寺の北山大塔は義満の命令で焼失した相国寺の七重大塔を移転再建したもので、義満の死後に金閣寺の北山大塔が焼失すると、今度は[[足利義持]]の意向で相国寺の元の場所で七重大塔が再建されたという経緯があるため、両者は同じ塔と言える<ref>原田正俊「相国寺の創建と足利義満の仏寺法会」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref><ref>冨島義幸「相国寺七重塔とその伽藍」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref>）。
{{-}}
==東大寺七重塔の復元== [[1970年]]、[[日本万国博覧会]]の古河パビリオンは、東大寺東塔を再現したものであった。このパビリオンは、博覧会終了後に取り壊されている。
東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>[http://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html 七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から] 産経新聞west（2013年4月26日）2017年2月11日 </ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== {{Commons|Category:Pagodas|仏塔}} {{Commons|Category:Pagodas in Japan|日本の仏塔}} *[[高層建築物]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[多宝塔]] *[[宝塔]] *[[五輪塔]] *[[宝篋印塔]] *[[パゴダ]] *[[相輪塔|相輪橖]] *[[宝珠]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ななしゆうのとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:ストゥーパ]]

'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|神奈川県[[海老名中央公園]]内に復元された相模国分寺の七重塔]]
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>{{Cite news|title=道鏡ゆかり、７０ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定|date=2017-2-9|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[読売新聞]]|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170212131248/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html|archivedate=2017-2-12}}</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ36[[丈]]（約109 m））が<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.shokoku-ji.jp/reference/relation/nana/|title=相国寺七重大塔|accessdate=2021-4-21|publisher=臨済宗相国寺派|work=関連資料}}</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>{{Cite news|title=金閣寺：敷地内から装飾品出土 七重塔「北山大塔」部材か|date=2016-7-8|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[毎日新聞]]|last=宮川|first=佐知子|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c}}</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した（なお、金閣寺の北山大塔は義満の命令で焼失した相国寺の七重大塔を移転再建したもので、義満の死後に金閣寺の北山大塔が焼失すると、今度は[[足利義持]]の意向で相国寺の元の場所で七重大塔が再建されたという経緯があるため、両者は同じ塔と言える<ref>原田正俊「相国寺の創建と足利義満の仏寺法会」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref><ref>冨島義幸「相国寺七重塔とその伽藍」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref>）。

'''七重塔'''（ななじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|神奈川県[[海老名中央公園]]内に復元された相模国分寺の七重塔]]
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>{{Cite news|title=道鏡ゆかり、７０ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定|date=2017-2-9|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[読売新聞]]|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170212131248/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html|archivedate=2017-2-12}}</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ36[[丈]]（約109 m））が<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.shokoku-ji.jp/reference/relation/nana/|title=相国寺七重大塔|accessdate=2021-4-21|publisher=臨済宗相国寺派|work=関連資料}}</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>{{Cite news|title=金閣寺：敷地内から装飾品出土 七重塔「北山大塔」部材か|date=2016-7-8|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[毎日新聞]]|last=宮川|first=佐知子|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c}}</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した（なお、金閣寺の北山大塔は義満の命令で焼失した相国寺の七重大塔を移転再建したもので、義満の死後に金閣寺の北山大塔が焼失すると、今度は[[足利義持]]の意向で相国寺の元の場所で七重大塔が再建されたという経緯があるため、両者は同じ塔と言える<ref>原田正俊「相国寺の創建と足利義満の仏寺法会」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref><ref>冨島義幸「相国寺七重塔とその伽藍」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref>）。
<gallery> ファイル:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[東大寺]]七重塔模型 ファイル:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[讃岐国分寺]]石造復元模型 ファイル:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|奈良・[[栄山寺]]石造七重塔（鎌倉時代、[[重要文化財]]） </gallery>
{{-}}
==東大寺七重塔の復元== [[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|中央の七重塔が[[古河財閥|古河]]パビリオン]][[日本万国博覧会]]の古河パビリオンは、東大寺東塔を再現したものであった。このパビリオンは、博覧会終了後に取り壊されている。
東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>{{Cite news|title=七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から|date=2013-4-26|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[産経新聞]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html}} </ref>。 {{-}} ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== {{Commons|Category:Pagodas|仏塔}} {{Commons|Category:Pagodas in Japan|日本の仏塔}} *[[高層建築物]] *[[仏塔]] *[[三重塔]] *[[多宝塔]] *[[宝塔]] *[[五輪塔]] *[[宝篋印塔]] *[[パゴダ]] *[[相輪橖]] *[[宝珠]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ななしゆうのとう}} [[Category:寺院建築]] [[Category:ストゥーパ]]

'''七重塔'''（しちじゅうのとう）は、[[仏塔]]の形式の一つ。層塔と呼ばれる楼閣形式の仏塔のうち、七重の屋根を持つものを指す。日本では、近世までに建造された大型木造七重塔で現存するものは無い。 ==概要== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|神奈川県[[海老名中央公園]]内に復元された相模国分寺の七重塔]]
仏塔の意味や位置づけ等は、[[五重塔]]の項を参照のこと。
日本では、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]により各地に[[国分寺]]が設置された際に、寺院とともに七重塔の建設が進められた。このため[[武蔵国分寺跡|武蔵国分寺]]、[[阿波国分寺]]、[[讃岐国分寺]]など各地の国分寺の遺構には、七重塔の基壇を含むものもある。[[東大寺]]においては、東塔、西塔の二本の七重塔が建設された。[[平安時代]]においては[[道鏡]]により由義寺（[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]）に七重塔が<ref>{{Cite news|title=道鏡ゆかり、７０ｍ級の七重塔跡…土台と断定|date=2017-2-9|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[読売新聞]]|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170212131248/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/culture/20170209-OYT1T50154.html|archivedate=2017-2-12}}</ref>、[[鎌倉時代]]には東大寺東塔の再建が、[[室町時代]]には[[足利義満]]により[[相国寺]]（[[京都市]]）に八角七重塔（高さ36[[丈]]（約109 m））が<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.shokoku-ji.jp/reference/relation/nana/|title=相国寺七重大塔|accessdate=2021-4-21|publisher=臨済宗相国寺派|work=関連資料}}</ref>、また、[[金閣寺]]付近には北山大塔が建設されたが<ref>{{Cite news|title=金閣寺：敷地内から装飾品出土 七重塔「北山大塔」部材か|date=2016-7-8|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[毎日新聞]]|last=宮川|first=佐知子|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160709/k00/00m/040/164000c}}</ref>、いずれも[[落雷]]や兵火により焼失した（なお、金閣寺の北山大塔は義満の命令で焼失した相国寺の七重大塔を移転再建したもので、義満の死後に金閣寺の北山大塔が焼失すると、今度は[[足利義持]]の意向で相国寺の元の場所で七重大塔が再建されたという経緯があるため、両者は同じ塔と言える<ref>原田正俊「相国寺の創建と足利義満の仏寺法会」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref><ref>冨島義幸「相国寺七重塔とその伽藍」桃崎有一郎・山田邦和 編著『室町政権の首府構想と京都-室町・北山・東山-』（文理閣、2016年） ISBN 978-4-89259-798-5</ref>）。
<gallery> ファイル:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[東大寺]]七重塔模型 ファイル:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[讃岐国分寺]]石造復元模型 ファイル:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|奈良・[[栄山寺]]石造七重塔（鎌倉時代、[[重要文化財]]） </gallery>
{{-}}

東大寺では、創建当初の伽藍復元に向けた整備基本構想があり、構成要素の一つである東塔の再現も視野に含まれているが、実際に建設する可否や時期等は発掘調査が進んでから具体化される見込み<ref>{{Cite news|title=七重塔復元へ基本構想 奈良・東大寺 整備は東塔地区から|date=2013-4-26|accessdate=2017-2-11|newspaper=[[産経新聞]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html}} </ref>。 {{-}} ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30922709]]
Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|qq=|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|qq=|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|qq=|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|qq=|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|qq=|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|qq=|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|qq=|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|qq=|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|qq=|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|qq=|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|qq=|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|qq=|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|qq=|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|qq=|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>

[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]]
Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has English label - based on Wikidata English label
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]]
Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>

In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>

[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.
In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)
<gallery> File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) </gallery>
{{-}}
==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda== [[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>
At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref> {{-}}
==Footnotes== {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]] Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.
==Overview== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]
For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.
In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)
<gallery> File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) </gallery>
{{-}}
==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda== [[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>
At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref> {{-}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]]
Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]] Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.
==Overview== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]
For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.
In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)
<gallery> File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) </gallery>
{{-}}
==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda== [[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>
At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref> {{-}}

During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)
<gallery> File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) </gallery>
{{-}}
==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda== [[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>
At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref> {{-}}
