revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]] Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of [[:en:Stupa|Buddhist Pagoda]]. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.
==Overview== [[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]
For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.
In Japan, when [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubunji]] throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and [[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]]—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] – [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]]) under the auspices of [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. In the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], the east pagoda of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] was rebuilt, and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], under the patronage of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]], a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]], approximately 109 m) was built at [[:en:Shōkoku-ji|Sōkoku-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Additionally, near [[:en:Kinkaku-ji|Kinkaku-ji]], a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning Strike]] or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimochi|Ashikaga Yoshimochi]], meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)
<gallery> File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|[[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]] seven‐tiered pagoda model File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|[[:en:Sanuki Kokubun-ji|Sanuki Kokubunji]] stone reconstruction model File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – [[:en:Eisan-ji|Eizan-ji]] stone seven‐tiered pagoda ([[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) </gallery>
{{-}}
==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda== [[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The [[:en:Expo '70|Japan World Exposition]]’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]].<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]]|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>
At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref> {{-}}

Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda}}. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubunji}} throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and {{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}}—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} – {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao}}) under the auspices of {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō}}. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, the east pagoda of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} was rebuilt, and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, under the patronage of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 {{ill|Shaku (unit)|en|Shaku (unit)|WD=Q931805|lt=shaku}}, approximately 109 m) was built at {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|WD=Q1786709|lt=Sōkoku-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Additionally, near {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|en|Kinkaku-ji|WD=Q270983|lt=Kinkaku-ji}}, a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by {{ill|Lightning strike|en|Lightning strike|WD=Q15120872|lt=Lightning Strike}} or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|en|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|WD=Q466287|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimochi}}, meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|{{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|{{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}} stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – {{ill|Eisan-ji|en|Eisan-ji|WD=Q11535667|lt=Eizan-ji}} stone seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}})
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30922709]]
Wikidata name: seven-storied pagoda
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda}}. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubunji}} throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and {{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}}—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} – {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao}}) under the auspices of {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō}}. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, the east pagoda of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} was rebuilt, and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, under the patronage of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 {{ill|Shaku (unit)|en|Shaku (unit)|WD=Q931805|lt=shaku}}, approximately 109 m) was built at {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|WD=Q1786709|lt=Sōkoku-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Additionally, near {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|en|Kinkaku-ji|WD=Q270983|lt=Kinkaku-ji}}, a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by {{ill|Lightning strike|en|Lightning strike|WD=Q15120872|lt=Lightning Strike}} or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|en|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|WD=Q466287|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimochi}}, meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|{{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|{{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}} stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – {{ill|Eisan-ji|en|Eisan-ji|WD=Q11535667|lt=Eizan-ji}} stone seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}})
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>

In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, the east pagoda of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} was rebuilt, and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, under the patronage of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 {{ill|Shaku (unit)|en|Shaku (unit)|WD=Q931805|lt=shaku}}, approximately 109 m) was built at {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|WD=Q1786709|lt=Sōkoku-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Additionally, near {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|en|Kinkaku-ji|WD=Q270983|lt=Kinkaku-ji}}, a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by {{ill|Lightning strike|en|Lightning strike|WD=Q15120872|lt=Lightning Strike}} or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|en|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|WD=Q466287|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimochi}}, meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|{{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|{{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}} stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – {{ill|Eisan-ji|en|Eisan-ji|WD=Q11535667|lt=Eizan-ji}} stone seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}})
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]

'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda}}. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Ebina Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubunji}} throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and {{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}}—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} – {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao}}) under the auspices of {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō}}. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, the east pagoda of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} was rebuilt, and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, under the patronage of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 {{ill|Shaku (unit)|en|Shaku (unit)|WD=Q931805|lt=shaku}}, approximately 109 m) was built at {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|WD=Q1786709|lt=Sōkoku-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Additionally, near {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|en|Kinkaku-ji|WD=Q270983|lt=Kinkaku-ji}}, a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by {{ill|Lightning strike|en|Lightning strike|WD=Q15120872|lt=Lightning Strike}} or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|en|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|WD=Q466287|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimochi}}, meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|{{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}} seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|{{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji}} stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – {{ill|Eisan-ji|en|Eisan-ji|WD=Q11535667|lt=Eizan-ji}} stone seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period}}, {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}})
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda|1=Stupa}}. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Q11559143|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=Five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and {{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}}—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}, two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} – {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao|1=Yao, Osaka}}) under the auspices of {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō|1=Dōkyō}}. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period|1=Kamakura period}}, the east pagoda of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} was rebuilt, and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period|1=Muromachi period}}, under the patronage of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 {{ill|Shaku (unit)|en|Shaku (unit)|WD=Q931805|lt=shaku|1=Shaku (unit)}}, approximately 109 m) was built at {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|WD=Q1786709|lt=Sōkoku-ji|1=Shōkoku-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. Additionally, near {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|en|Kinkaku-ji|WD=Q270983|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=Kinkaku-ji}}, a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by {{ill|Lightning strike|en|Lightning strike|WD=Q15120872|lt=Lightning Strike|1=Lightning strike}} or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|en|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|WD=Q466287|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimochi|1=Ashikaga Yoshimochi}}, meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition|1=Expo '70}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei|1=The Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun|1=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Completion tier 1]] (crud category cleanup)
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七重塔]]

'''Shichijūtō''' (七重塔) is one form of {{ill|Stupa|en|Stupa|WD=Q180987|lt=Buddhist Pagoda|1=Stupa}}. It refers to the tower‐type Buddhist pagodas that feature seven roofs. In Japan, no large wooden seven‐tiered pagoda built until the early modern period still exists.

==Overview==
[[Image:Sagami Provincial Temple.jpg|200px|right|thumb|In Kanagawa Prefecture, the seven‐tiered pagoda of the Sagami Kokubunji was reconstructed within {{ill|Ebina Central Park|de|Ebina-Zentralpark|ja|海老名中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina Central Park|1=Q11559143|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559143}}]]

For the meaning and significance of Buddhist pagodas, see the article on {{ill|Five-tiered Pagoda|de|Fünfstöckige Pagode|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-tiered Pagoda|1=Five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}.

In Japan, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} issued an {{ill|Imperial Decree|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=Command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q905765}} to establish {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubunji|1=Provincial temple}} throughout the country, the construction of seven‐tiered pagodas advanced alongside temple building. Consequently, at the remains of various Kokubunji—such as {{ill|Musashi Kokubunji|de|Musashi Kokubunji|ja|武蔵国分寺跡|lt=Musashi Kokubunji|lt_ja=武蔵国分寺|lt_de=Musashi Kokubunji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Kokubunji|1=Musashi Kokubun-ji Remains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546338}}, {{ill|Awa Kokubunji|de|Awa Kokubunji|ja|阿波国分寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kokubunji|1=Awa Kokubun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657516}}, and {{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}}—some of the temple ruins include the base platform of a seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Pagoda Base|de|Pagodenbasis|ja|基壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pagoda Base|1=kidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124607026}}). At {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}, two seven‐tiered pagodas (one in the east and one in the west) were constructed. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda was built at Yūgiji in ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} – {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao|1=Yao, Osaka}}) under the auspices of {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō|1=Dōkyō}}. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period|1=Kamakura period}}, the east pagoda of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} was rebuilt, and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period|1=Muromachi period}}, under the patronage of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, a seven‐tiered pagoda (with a height of 36 {{ill|Shaku (unit)|en|Shaku (unit)|WD=Q931805|lt=shaku|1=Shaku (unit)}}, approximately 109 m) was built at {{ill|Shōkoku-ji|en|Shōkoku-ji|WD=Q1786709|lt=Sōkoku-ji|1=Shōkoku-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. Additionally, near {{ill|Kinkaku-ji|en|Kinkaku-ji|WD=Q270983|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=Kinkaku-ji}}, a pagoda known as the Kitayama Great Pagoda was constructed; however, all these structures were subsequently destroyed by {{ill|Lightning strike|en|Lightning strike|WD=Q15120872|lt=Lightning Strike|1=Lightning strike}} or warfare. (In fact, the Kitayama Great Pagoda near Kinkaku-ji was a reconstruction—relocated from the destroyed seven‐tiered pagoda of Sōkoku-ji, which had been razed by order of Yoshimitsu. After its subsequent destruction near Kinkaku-ji following Yoshimitsu’s death, a seven‐tiered pagoda was rebuilt at the original Sōkoku-ji site at the behest of {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|en|Ashikaga Yoshimochi|WD=Q466287|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimochi|1=Ashikaga Yoshimochi}}, meaning that both structures can be regarded as the same pagoda.)

<gallery>
File:Todaiji model pagoda.jpg|{{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} seven‐tiered pagoda model
File:讃岐国分寺 石造復元模型.JPG|{{ill|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|en|Sanuki Kokubun-ji|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}} stone reconstruction model
File:Eisanji sekitou.JPG|Nara – {{ill|Eisan-ji|en|Eisan-ji|WD=Q11535667|lt=Eizan-ji|1=Eisan-ji}} stone seven‐tiered pagoda ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura Period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})
</gallery>

{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition|1=Expo '70}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei|1=The Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun|1=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
{{-}}

==Restoration of Tōdai-ji Seven-tiered Pagoda==
[[File:Furukawa Pavilion.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The central seven‐tiered pagoda is represented by {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}}]] The {{ill|Expo '70|en|Expo '70|WD=Q1119956|lt=Japan World Exposition|1=Expo '70}}’s {{ill|Furukawa Pavilion|de|Furukawa Pavillon|ja|古河パビリオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Pavilion|1=Furukawa Pavilion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131411475}} was a reproduction of the Tōdai-ji seven‐tiered pagoda. After the exposition ended, the pavilion was demolished, and only the pagoda finial was relocated to the east side of the Great Buddha Hall at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Reconstructed Aspiration: The Finial Etched in History – 1970, Osaka; Reproduction of Tōdai-ji’s Seven-tiered Pagoda|date=2019-05-28|accessdate=2019-05-28|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei|1=The Nikkei}}|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210805033312/https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO45318150X20C19A5AA1P00/|archivedate=2021-08-05|url-status=live}}</ref>

At Tōdai-ji, there is a master plan for restoring the original temple complex, which includes the reconstruction of the east pagoda. However, the feasibility and timing of actual construction will be determined once further archaeological investigations have progressed.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Basic Plan Outlined for Seven-tiered Pagoda Restoration – Restoration work to begin in the east pagoda district at Tōdai-ji in Nara|date=2013-04-26|accessdate=2017-02-11|newspaper={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun|1=Sankei Shimbun}}|url=https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|deadlinkdate=2023-09-23|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422180951/https://www.sankei.com/west/news/130426/wst1304260032-n1.html|archivedate=2021-04-22}}</ref>
